meta.title: Original AC Practice Question Bank
meta.chapter: NCERT Class 12 Physics Chapter 7 - Alternating Current
meta.licenseNote: Original generated educational questions. No copied coaching or previous-year content.

questions.commonTrap: Using half-cycle average instead of RMS.
questions.shortcut: RMS = 0.707 x peak.
questions.question: A sinusoidal AC voltage has peak value 30 V. Its RMS value is closest to
questions.solution: For sinusoidal AC, Vrms = V0/sqrt(2) = 30/sqrt(2) = 21.21 V.

questions.commonTrap: Treating meter reading as peak value.
questions.shortcut: Peak = 1.414 x RMS.
questions.question: An AC ammeter reads 15 A for a sinusoidal current. The peak current is
questions.solution: AC ammeters read RMS. I0 = Irms sqrt(2) = 15sqrt(2) = 21.21 A.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting omega = 2 pi f.
questions.shortcut: Coefficient of pi in omega divided by 2 gives f.
questions.question: For v = V0 sin(60 pi t), the frequency of AC is
questions.solution: Compare omega = 60 pi with omega = 2 pi f. Hence f = 60/2 = 30 Hz.

questions.commonTrap: Using capacitive reactance formula for inductor.
questions.shortcut: Inductive reactance grows directly with f and L.
questions.question: The inductive reactance of a 0.05 H inductor at 50 Hz is closest to
questions.solution: XL = 2 pi f L = 2 pi x 50 x 0.05 = 15.71 ohm.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting micro = 10^-6.
questions.shortcut: Convert microfarad to farad before calculating XC.
questions.question: A capacitor of 20 microF is connected to 50 Hz AC. Its capacitive reactance is closest to
questions.solution: XC = 1/(2 pi f C). Convert 20 microF to 20 x 10^-6 F, then substitute.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing lead and lag.
questions.shortcut: R together, L lag, C current comes first.
questions.question: In a pure inductor AC circuit, the correct phase relation is
options: Current and voltage are always opposite
questions.solution: Pure R has zero phase difference, pure L has current lagging by 90 degrees, and pure C has current leading by 90 degrees.

questions.commonTrap: Saying impedance is zero even when R is present.
questions.shortcut: Resonance = reactance cancellation.
questions.question: At resonance in a series LCR circuit, which statement is correct?
options: XL = XC and current is maximum
questions.answer: XL = XC and current is maximum
questions.solution: At resonance, inductive and capacitive reactances cancel, Z = R and current becomes maximum.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing turns ratio.
questions.shortcut: Voltage follows turns.
questions.question: An ideal transformer has Np = 100, Ns = 150 and Vp = 120 V. The secondary voltage is
questions.solution: Vs/Vp = Ns/Np, so Vs = 120 x 150/100 = 180 V.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting the power factor.
questions.shortcut: Average AC power = apparent power x power factor.
questions.question: An AC circuit has Vrms = 120 V, Irms = 2 A and power factor 0.55. Average power is
questions.solution: P = VI cos phi = 120 x 2 x 0.55 = 132 W.

questions.commonTrap: Using full-cycle average zero.
questions.shortcut: Half-cycle mean = 0.637 x peak.
questions.question: The mean value over a positive half cycle of i = 12 sin omega t ampere is
questions.solution: For a sine wave, half-cycle mean = 2I0/pi = 2 x 12/pi = 7.64 A.

questions.solution: P = VI cos phi = 280 x 2 x 0.7 = 392 W.

questions.commonTrap: Using full-cycle average zero.
questions.shortcut: Half-cycle mean = 0.637 x peak.
questions.question: The mean value over a positive half cycle of i = 28 sin omega t ampere is
questions.solution: For a sine wave, half-cycle mean = 2I0/pi = 2 x 28/pi = 17.83 A.

questions.commonTrap: Using half-cycle average instead of RMS.
questions.shortcut: RMS = 0.707 x peak.
questions.question: A sinusoidal AC voltage has peak value 120 V. Its RMS value is closest to
questions.solution: For sinusoidal AC, Vrms = V0/sqrt(2) = 120/sqrt(2) = 84.85 V.

questions.commonTrap: Treating meter reading as peak value.
questions.shortcut: Peak = 1.414 x RMS.
questions.question: An AC ammeter reads 60 A for a sinusoidal current. The peak current is
questions.solution: AC ammeters read RMS. I0 = Irms sqrt(2) = 60sqrt(2) = 84.85 A.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting omega = 2 pi f.
questions.shortcut: Coefficient of pi in omega divided by 2 gives f.
questions.question: For v = V0 sin(240 pi t), the frequency of AC is
questions.solution: Compare omega = 240 pi with omega = 2 pi f. Hence f = 240/2 = 120 Hz.

questions.commonTrap: Using capacitive reactance formula for inductor.
questions.shortcut: Inductive reactance grows directly with f and L.
questions.question: The inductive reactance of a 0.5 H inductor at 50 Hz is closest to
questions.solution: XL = 2 pi f L = 2 pi x 50 x 0.5 = 157.08 ohm.

questions.commonTrap: Forgetting micro = 10^-6.
questions.shortcut: Convert microfarad to farad before calculating XC.
questions.question: A capacitor of 200 microF is connected to 50 Hz AC. Its capacitive reactance is closest to
questions.solution: XC = 1/(2 pi f C). Convert 200 microF to 200 x 10^-6 F, then substitute.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing lead and lag.
questions.shortcut: R together, L lag, C current comes first.
questions.question: In a pure inductor AC circuit, the correct phase relation is
options: Current and voltage are always opposite
questions.solution: Pure R has zero phase difference, pure L has current lagging by 90 degrees, and pure C has current leading by 90 degrees.

questions.commonTrap: Saying impedance is zero even when R is present.
questions.shortcut: Resonance = reactance cancellation.
questions.question: At resonance in a series LCR circuit, which statement is correct?
options: XL = XC and current is maximum
questions.answer: XL = XC and current is maximum
questions.solution: At resonance, inductive and capacitive reactances cancel, Z = R and current becomes maximum.

questions.solution: Vs/Vp = Ns/Np, so Vs = 300 x 600/100 = 1800 V.

questions.question: A series circuit has R, L and C. Is current same through all elements? Circuit set 4.
questions.answer: Yes, same RMS current flows through all series elements.
questions.solution: Series circuit has one path for current.

questions.commonTrap: Assigning different currents to series elements.
questions.shortcut: Identify element behavior before formula use.
questions.question: Which element should be used as a low-loss AC current limiter? Circuit set 4.
questions.solution: A choke uses inductive reactance with small resistance.

questions.commonTrap: Assigning different currents to series elements.
questions.shortcut: Identify element behavior before formula use.
questions.question: What is net nature if XL > XC? Circuit set 4.
questions.solution: Positive net reactance means current lags voltage.

questions.commonTrap: Assigning different currents to series elements.
questions.shortcut: Identify element behavior before formula use.
questions.question: Why is resistor power non-zero in AC? Circuit set 4.
questions.answer: Because heat depends on i^2, whose average is not zero.
questions.solution: Positive and negative current both heat the resistor.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing lead and lag when reference changes.
questions.shortcut: Take current as reference unless stated otherwise.
questions.question: In pure L phasor diagram with current as reference, where is voltage? Phasor set 1.
questions.solution: Inductor voltage leads current.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing lead and lag when reference changes.
questions.shortcut: Take current as reference unless stated otherwise.
questions.question: In pure C phasor diagram with current as reference, where is voltage? Phasor set 1.
questions.solution: Capacitor current leads voltage, so voltage lags current.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing lead and lag when reference changes.
questions.shortcut: Take current as reference unless stated otherwise.
questions.question: What side of impedance triangle gives power factor? Phasor set 1.
questions.solution: Power factor is adjacent over hypotenuse.

questions.commonTrap: Reversing lead and lag when reference changes.
questions.shortcut: Take current as reference unless stated otherwise.
questions.question: What does vertical current component represent in wattless-current diagram? Phasor set 1.
questions.solution: It is perpendicular to voltage and gives no average power.

questions.commonTrap: Using XL + XC instead of XL - XC.
questions.shortcut: LCR case questions usually follow XL-XC -> Z -> I -> phi/P.
questions.caseText: A student studies a series LCR circuit connected to a 200 V RMS AC source. For setting 1, R = 30 ohm, XL = 80 ohm and XC = 40 ohm.
questions.question: Answer the subparts based on the circuit data.
questions.answer: Net reactance 40 ohm, Z 50 ohm, current 4 A.
questions.solution: Use the standard LCR chain: reactance difference, impedance triangle, then current.
